context: Mean age:  8 years Associated with:   endocrinopathy (in 2-3%) ◊  2-3% of patients with polyostotic fibrous dysplasia have McCune-Albright syndrome Location:   usually unilateral + asymmetric; femur (91%), tibia (81%), pelvis (78%), foot (73%), ribs (55%), skull + facial bones (50%), upper extremities, lumbar spine (14%), clavicle (10%), cervical spine (7%) Site:     metadiaphysis Rules: √  leg length discrepancy (70%) √  "shepherd's crook" deformity (35%) = coxa vara angulation of proximal femur √  "saber shin" deformity = anterior bowing of tibia √  facial asymmetry √  tibial bowing √  rib d
